servinfo.title: Front Brake Caliper Disassembly and Reassembly

attention2.ptxt: Take care not to damage piston and caliper cylinder of front brake caliper.

attention1.ptxt: Do not apply highly compressed air to the piston as it is. Place a cloth (1) to prevent brake piston from jumping-out. Gradually apply compressed air. Do not place your fingers in front of brake piston while applying compressed air.

s1.ptxt: Clean surfaces around front brake caliper with brake cleaner before disassembly.
Remove brake piston by blowing air into the hole for front brake flexible hose joint bolt.
Remove piston boot (1) and bleeder plug (2).
Remove piston seal (1) from cylinder (3) of front brake caliper (3) using flat-bladed screwdriver (2) or the like.

topic.title: Slide pin
topic.ptxt: Remove slide pin (1) and pin boot (2) from front brake caliper carrier (3).

attention2.ptxt: Use only the specified brake fluid to wash brake parts. Any other fluids or solvents are detrimental to the parts.

attention3.ptxt: Do not reuse rubber parts (piston seal, piston boot and pin boot).

s1.ptxt: Perform the following steps before installing brake piston to front brake caliper.

s2.ptxt: Check entire periphery of brake piston and inside surface of cylinder for rust, corrosion and any other damage. If check result is not OK, replace front brake caliper.
After applying rubber grease included in piston seal set of supply parts or specified brake fluid to new piston seal (1), fit the seal into groove in cylinder taking care not to twist the seal.

spec.title: Brake fluid
spec.ptxt: Refer to reservoir cap of brake master cylinder or owner’s manual.

s2.ptxt: Apply rubber grease included in piston seal set of supply parts or specified brake fluid to new piston boot (1). And then install it onto brake piston as shown.

entry.ptxt: Double-grooved end on brake pad side

s1.ptxt: Fit piston boot (1) with piston into boot groove in cylinder by hand as shown below.
Insert brake piston (1) into cylinder by hand and fit piston boot (2) in boot groove on brake piston.

attention3.ptxt: Check that piston boot is securely fitted in boot grooves in brake piston and cylinder.

s1.ptxt: To confirm that piston boot (2) is fitted in its groove in cylinder properly, pull brake piston (1) a little outward without moving it fully out.
Insert brake piston into cylinder by hand.
Install bleeder plug to front brake caliper and tighten it to specified torque.
Check the following points before installing slide pin to front brake caliper carrier.

s2.ptxt: Check slide pins for rust, corrosion and other damage. Replace slide pin if defective.
Apply rubber grease to in side of new pin boot (1).
Install pin boots (1) to front brake caliper carrier (2) with their ends directed as shown below.

attention3.ptxt: Use rubber grease whose viscosity varies very little even at –40°C (–40°F).

s2.ptxt: Apply rubber grease to slide pin (1).

s1.ptxt: Install two slide pins (1) with three grooves, or slide pin (1) with three grooves and slide pin (2) without groove, to front brake caliper carrier as shown in figure.
